note_data.title: Capacitance
keyPoints: One radian is defined as the angle in the sector of a circle when the arc length equals the radius
Angular speed (ω) = v/r = 2π/T = 2πf, where v is linear speed and r is radius
Linear speed v = ωr (product of angular speed and radius)
Objects in circular motion have constantly changing velocity due to changing direction, requiring centripetal acceleration
Centripetal force always acts perpendicular to motion towards the centre of the circle
Centripetal force F = mv²/r = mω²r using Newton's second law

definitions.definition: The angle in the sector of a circle when the arc length of that sector is equal to the radius of the circle
The angle turned through by an object in any given direction in radians
The angle an object moves through per unit time
The acceleration of an object moving in a circular path at constant speed due to constantly changing velocity
The resultant force that acts perpendicular to the motion of an object towards the centre of the circle

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Start with the given equation and manipulate algebraically to reach the required result
Show all working steps with correct units and appropriate significant figures

modelAnswer.question: A 470 μF capacitor is charged to 12 V and then discharged through a 2.2 kΩ resistor. Calculate (a) the initial energy stored (b) the time constant (c) the charge remaining after 2.0 s.

commonMistakes: Confusing degrees and radians - remember to convert using π/180
Thinking objects in uniform circular motion don't accelerate because speed is constant
Incorrectly stating centripetal force acts tangentially rather than towards the centre
Mixing up the formulas for centripetal acceleration: a = v²/r vs a = ω²r
examinerTips: Remember that velocity has both magnitude and direction - constant speed in circular motion means changing velocity
Always check units when using angular measurements - ensure radians are used in calculations
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Exponential decay graph showing charge Q against time t, with time constant τ marked where Q = Q₀/e, and axes clearly labelled with Q₀ at t = 0
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q4 (6 marks) — they asked students to calculate the energy stored in a parallel plate capacitor and explain how capacitance changes when dielectric is inserted
This topic appeared in O/N 2022 P2 Q3 (5 marks) — students had to derive the formula for capacitors in series and calculate equivalent capacitance
This topic was examined in M/J 2022 P1 Q12 (1 mark) — multiple choice question on factors affecting capacitance of parallel plate capacitor
This topic was tested in O/N 2023 P2 Q5 (7 marks) — comprehensive question involving capacitor charging through resistor and energy calculations

workedExample.question: A 4.7 μF capacitor is charged to 12 V. Calculate (a) the charge stored (b) the energy stored. The capacitor is then connected in parallel with an uncharged 2.2 μF capacitor. Calculate (c) the final voltage across both capacitors. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Calculate initial charge using Q = CV = 4.7 × 10⁻⁶ × 12 = 5.64 × 10⁻⁵ C
Step 2: Calculate initial energy using E = ½CV² = ½ × 4.7 × 10⁻⁶ × 12² = 3.38 × 10⁻⁴ J
Step 3: When connected in parallel, total capacitance = 4.7 + 2.2 = 6.9 μF
Step 4: Total charge remains constant (5.64 × 10⁻⁵ C), so final voltage = Q/C_total = 5.64 × 10⁻⁵ / 6.9 × 10⁻⁶ = 8.17 V
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for correct formula and substitution in (a), 1 mark for answer in (a), 1 mark for correct formula in (b), 1 mark for answer in (b), 1 mark for recognizing charge conservation and calculating total capacitance in (c), 1 mark for final voltage calculation

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused the formulas for series and parallel combinations' — always remember that for capacitors in parallel C_total = C₁ + C₂, opposite to resistors
Examiner Report 2022: 'Students frequently forgot to convert units correctly' — always convert μF to F (multiply by 10⁻⁶) before substituting into formulas
Examiner Report 2023: 'Common error was stating that energy is conserved when capacitors are connected' — remember that charge is conserved, but energy decreases when capacitors redistribute charge
note_data.keyDiagram: Students must be able to draw a parallel plate capacitor showing two parallel conducting plates separated by distance d, with area A marked, electric field lines drawn perpendicular between plates, and ability to label the dielectric material between plates. Include circuit symbol (two parallel lines) and show how to represent capacitors in series and parallel combinations in circuit diagrams.
note_data.quickRecap: Circular motion involves angular displacement measured in radians, where one radian equals the angle when arc length equals radius. Objects in uniform circular motion experience centripetal acceleration (a = v²/r = ω²r) due to constantly changing velocity direction. This requires centripetal force (F = mv²/r = mω²r) acting perpendicular to motion towards the circle's centre.
